subject: [RFC PATCH v2 3/7] mm, swap: support physical swap as a vswap backend

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned whether calling folio_realloc_swap() without vswap_zswap_load() first is necessary and suggested modifying the realloc_swap API to take a swpentry directly, loading it within if needed.
reviews.raw_body:
I didn't look through the rest of the series, but are there use cases
for calling folio_realloc_swap() without calling vswap_zswap_load()
first? I wonder if the realloc_swap API should take the swpentry
directly and do the load within? Something like
vswap_alloc_phys(swpentry, folio)?

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapout fallback path also uses physical swap allocation and agreed to avoid re-running zswap_load(), no fix planned.
reviews.raw_body:
It's also use on the swapout fallback path! If zswap rejects the page
or is disabled, and memory.zswap.writeback=1, then we allocate phys
swap space. We probably don't wanna do zswap_load() there again :)

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested abstracting xarray operations and vswap operations into helper functions, specifically zswap_tree_store() and zswap_tree_load(), to reduce code divergence and improve maintainability.

Reviewer Yosry Ahmed suggested abstracting away the distinction between xarray and vswap in the code, specifically proposing a helper function to remove zswap entries from vswap without acquiring locks, and expressed uncertainty about whether this is necessary or if there's a better approach.
reviews.raw_body:
There's a lot of divergence in the code (in this patch and previous
ones). Seems like a lot of it is to do xarray operations vs vswap
operations. I wonder if we can abstract these into helpers, e.g.
zswap_tree_store(), zswap_tree_load(), etc. Maybe the name is not the
best, but you get the point :)

Here we can then do zswap_tree_load() for both code paths and only the
folio_realloc_swap() needs to be different for vswap. We can do
similar cleanups for the load/store paths as well.

---

Maybe this can also be abstracted into a helper, but I wonder what the
corresponding vswap operation would be. I think folio_realloc_swap()
will have already "erased" the zswap entry from vswap. Maybe have a
vswap helper that will only remove it if it's a zswap entry? We can
probably do a lockless check first to make it cheap?

It's probably silly to do this, and maybe there's a better way.
Generally, I think the code would be easier to follow if we abstract
away the xarray vs. vswap stuff into helpers (where it's reasonable).

2026-06-23.patch_summary: This patch adds support for physical swap as a backend for the virtual swap layer (vswap). It allows vswap to allocate a physical slot on demand when needed, serving as a fallback for zswap_store failures or as the destination for zswap writeback. The physical slot is tracked via a Pointer-tagged swap_table entry on the physical cluster, referencing the vswap entry. This patch modifies various kernel files, including mm/memory.c, mm/page_io.c, and mm/vmscan.c, to enable this functionality.

reviews.author: Nhat Pham (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged Yosry Ahmed's feedback about the swapoff path needing to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreeing that the logic is correct and no change is needed.

Author acknowledged a potential issue with erasing swap entries being asymmetric between vswap and non-vswap cases, but didn't commit to addressing it.
reviews.raw_body:
Yup that's the right logic. We already change the backend to physical
swap slot here, so there's no real "erase".

---

I'm not entirely sure if its worth it either, yeah. Unlike load and
store, erase seems a bit asymmetric in the sense that we only need to
do it for non-vswap cases.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed expressed mild concern about the lack of an erase operation in the vswap case, suggesting that a comment explaining this omission would be helpful.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah :/

Maybe just add a comment why no erase is needed for the vswap case.

2026-06-24.patch_summary: This patch adds support for physical swap as a backend for the virtual swap layer (vswap). It allows vswap to allocate a physical slot on demand when needed, serving as a fallback for zswap_store failures or as the destination for zswap writeback. The physical slot is tracked via a Pointer-tagged swap_table entry on the physical cluster, referencing the vswap entry. This patch modifies various kernel files, including mm/memory.c, mm/page_io.c, and mm/vmscan.c, to enable this functionality.
